input_excerpt: I led my fire team when our vehicle broke down during a convoy. I diagnosed the electrical fault myself, directed two Marines on the repair, and we rejoined the convoy within forty minutes.

findings.title: The technical work is stated, not shown
findings.quote: I diagnosed the electrical fault myself
findings.why: The employer is testing technical competence and problem-solving under pressure. "Diagnosed the electrical fault" is a conclusion, not a method — a civilian technical interviewer cannot tell what you checked, how you isolated the fault, or what skill you applied. Without the diagnostic steps, the reader cannot judge whether the problem was genuinely difficult or how you reasoned through it.
findings.task: Add what symptom you observed, what you ruled out, and how you located the specific fault, so the reader can see your diagnostic process. Do not change the outcome.

findings.title: The "pressure" the question asks about is missing
findings.quote: when our vehicle broke down during a convoy
findings.why: The question specifically asks about solving a problem *under pressure*. You name the setting but never establish the stakes — what was at risk if the vehicle stayed down, what constraints (time, safety, exposure) you worked against. Without stakes, "forty minutes" has no reference point and the reader cannot see why this was hard.
findings.task: State the specific pressure you faced (e.g., what the delay or breakdown threatened) so the reader understands why the situation was difficult, using only facts you can defend.

findings.title: Military term needs a civilian reference
findings.why: A corporate reader may not know a fire team's size or your scope of authority. Left unexplained, it obscures how many people you led and what "led" involved here versus the two Marines you separately directed on the repair.
findings.task: Briefly indicate the size of the group and your role relative to it in plain terms, without inflating your responsibility.

findings.title: Result lacks a comparison point
findings.quote: we rejoined the convoy within forty minutes
findings.why: The employer wants a defensible result. "Forty minutes" reads as fast or slow only if the reader knows the normal expectation. As written, the number is present but its significance is invisible.
findings.task: If you can defend it, note what a typical repair of this kind would have taken or why forty minutes mattered, so the result demonstrates impact.

note: A concise answer with clear ownership and a result, but the diagnostic method, the pressure, and the significance of the outcome are all asserted rather than shown.
